title: Supersede: metadata inheritance + propagation sweep + capture stubs (D2/D6)
action: Per CONTEXT D2 and D6: `decisions supersede` accepts --tags/--scope, inheriting BOTH from the superseded target when omitted (metadata-less target -> target scope or 'repo'). RED-FIRST: extend test_decisions_propagation.mjs with the new checks FIRST, run red against unmodified code, record via cells verify, then implement. ORDERING (lock doctrine): compute the propagation sweep BEFORE appending — sweep result rides the single supersede append; never a post-append rewrite of an already-written jsonl line. Sweep: recursively scan docs/** text files (md/json/yaml/txt) for the superseded id — full uuid AND short8 with word-boundary regex (no hex false positives) — collect {file, line, excerpt<=160ch}. Print the hit list (human + --json); the appended supersede event carries `sweep` {scanned_at, hit_count, files[]}; append one capture stub per hit via the capture module (source "supersede-sweep", outcome naming file:line + the new decision id) so unreconciled hits surface at every flush. Zero hits prints a clean no-citations line. Mirror to .bee/bin, tests (inheritance, sweep hit/no-hit, short8 word-boundary negative, stub creation, sweep-rides-the-append), render + manifest.

truths: supersede inherits tags/scope from target when flags omitted
sweep computed BEFORE the append — the event is written exactly once, already carrying sweep
sweep finds full-id and short8 citations under docs/** and writes one stub per hit
short8 embedded in a longer hex string does NOT match
RED-FIRST: failing run against unmodified code recorded as red evidence before implementation
prohibitions: Sweep never edits the citing files itself
Sweep scope is docs/** only (CONTEXT pinned; .bee/spikes excluded)
Never rewrite an already-appended event line

trace.outcome: Supersede propagation shipped: tags/scope inheritance, docs/** citation sweep riding the single append, one capture stub per hit
